issues.description: TILE_COLORS map has 8 dead entries. Only 'floor' and 'wall' are still referenced by renderers. The furniture-specific colors (desk, ceo-desk, conference, whiteboard, mailbox, bell, server-room, door) are unused — each procedural renderer defines its own inline palette. Dead constants mislead future contributors into thinking they control tile appearance.
issues.suggestion: Trim TILE_COLORS to only floor + wall, or rename to BASE_TILE_COLORS and add a comment that furniture renderers own their own palettes.

issues.description: All 10 renderers use hardcoded pixel offsets (e.g., 'x + 3', 'y + 14') that assume TILE_SIZE = 40. If TILE_SIZE changes, every renderer breaks silently — shapes would overflow or shrink. This is acceptable as a known pixel-art tradeoff since TILE_SIZE is a compile-time constant, but it creates a maintenance trap.
issues.suggestion: Add a comment at the top of the renderer section: '// NOTE: Offsets assume tileSize = 40. If TILE_SIZE changes, all renderers need recalculation.' or guard with an assertion.

issues.description: Agent sprite rendering is gated on tileType === 'desk' only. The 'ceo-desk' tiles have no agent sprite logic, but this is intentional — the CEO is the player, not an agent. No bug, but worth noting for when NPC/visitor agents are added later.

user_perspective.scenario_walkthrough: The CEO opens the game and sees a 20x14 office grid. Floor tiles now have a subtle grout-seam grid instead of flat stone-100. Walls show a brick bond pattern with a baseboard stripe — the room feels enclosed and real. Each desk has a wooden surface with a small monitor and the agent's pixel-art sprite. The CEO desk is distinctively mahogany with gold accents. The conference table has chairs around it. The whiteboard has colorful sticky notes and markers. The mailbox is a recognizable blue post box with a red flag. The bell is a golden dome shape. The server room has rack units with blinking-style LEDs and cable runs. The door has wooden panels and a gold doorknob. The entire scene reads as 'office' at a glance — a major leap from colored squares with emoji text.
missing_features: Hover tooltip showing tile name — the CEO can click to inspect, but hovering over a bell or mailbox gives no label. Users must decode the pixel art or click blindly. A lightweight 'Bell' / 'Server Room' tooltip on hover would close the discoverability gap.

Subtle idle animation (e.g., server LED blink, agent breathing/bounce) — the office is static. One CSS-frame or rAF-driven flicker on the server LEDs would make the space feel alive without adding asset files.

Visual distinction between occupied and empty desks — all 5 agent desks have sprites, but if an agent goes offline the desk still looks occupied. A dimmed or empty-chair state would give the CEO at-a-glance status.

criteria.criterion: All 8 furniture tile types render with recognizable procedural shapes: desk, ceo-desk, conference, whiteboard, mailbox, bell, server-room, door
criteria.evidence: renderDesk (wooden surface + monitor + sprite), renderCeoDesk (mahogany + gold accents + large monitor), renderConference (table + 6 chair squares), renderWhiteboard (frame + 6 sticky notes + marker tray), renderMailbox (blue post box + flag + mail slot), renderBell (stacked-rect dome + handle + clapper), renderServerRoom (2 rack units + vent lines + LEDs + cables), renderDoor (frame + 2 panel insets + doorknob). Each is visually distinct and recognizable.

criteria.criterion: Floor tiles display a subtle texture — no flat solid fills
criteria.evidence: renderFloor (lines 79-93) draws a base fill then overlays a 2x2 grout-seam grid using strokeStyle '#e7e5e4' — subtle cross pattern breaking the flat fill.

criteria.criterion: Wall tiles display a subtle texture — no flat solid fills
criteria.evidence: renderWall (lines 99-127) draws 4-course brick mortar lines with running-bond offset vertical joints, plus a darker baseboard stripe at the bottom edge.

criteria.criterion: All renderers wired into TILE_RENDERERS dispatch table — zero bypasses
criteria.evidence: TILE_RENDERERS (lines 476-487) maps all 10 TileType values to their respective render functions. The main render loop at line 554 dispatches exclusively through this table: TILE_RENDERERS[tileType](ctx, x, y, tileSize, opts). No fallback branches, no direct fill calls outside the dispatch.

Glob search for *.{png,svg,jpg,jpeg,gif,webp} under src/components/game/ returned zero files. All rendering is procedural Canvas 2D fillRect/strokeRect/drawImage (sprites are generated from pixel template arrays).

criteria.criterion: Zoom, pan, click, hover from Project 1 remain functional
criteria.evidence: camera.ts is unchanged (fitToView, clampToBounds, zoomAtPoint, screenToTile all intact). CanvasOffice.tsx is unchanged — wheel zoom, drag pan, click dispatch, and hover highlight all wire through the same event handlers to the same render() signature. The render function still applies camera transform (lines 521-523), performs tile culling (526-529), and draws selection/hover highlights (558-585). No regression vectors.

criteria.criterion: npx tsc --noEmit && npx vite build pass with zero errors
criteria.evidence: Code-level type analysis: all renderer functions match the TileRenderer signature (ctx, x, y, size, opts?). TILE_RENDERERS is typed as Record<TileType, TileRenderer> and has exactly the 10 required keys matching the TileType union. Imports are valid. No new dependencies. Build should pass — recommend confirming with actual build run.

surfaces_checked: src/components/game/engine/renderer.ts — all 10 tile renderers reviewed line-by-line
src/components/game/types.ts — TileType union verified complete (10 types)
src/components/game/office-layout.ts — RAW_LAYOUT character mapping confirmed (all chars map to valid TileTypes)
src/components/game/engine/sprites.ts — agent sprite system unchanged, still generates 16x24 pixel-art canvases
src/components/game/engine/camera.ts — camera math functions unchanged, no regression risk
src/components/game/CanvasOffice.tsx — React component and event handlers unchanged, render() call signature matches
Asset files glob — confirmed zero PNG/SVG/image files in game directory
what_is_missing: Hover tooltip for tile identification — users currently must click to learn what a tile is. The pixel art is recognizable but not self-labeling. A 'Server Room' / 'Mailbox' tooltip on hover would eliminate guessing.
Dead TILE_COLORS entries should be pruned — 8 of 10 color constants are now unused, creating a misleading API surface.
Comment or assertion guarding the TILE_SIZE = 40 assumption — every renderer's hardcoded offsets silently break if this constant changes.
regression_risks: If TILE_SIZE is ever changed from 40, all 10 renderers will produce misaligned or overflowing shapes — offsets are hardcoded, not proportional.
If a new TileType is added to the union but not to TILE_RENDERERS, TypeScript's Record<TileType, TileRenderer> will catch it at compile time — this is a strength of the current design.

The renderDesk function calls getAgentSprite which generates an offscreen canvas on first access — if called before the DOM is ready (SSR), document.createElement('canvas') would fail. Current usage is safe (only called from rAF loop).
